Block.Para: An Act to amend the Currency Act and the Royal Canadian Mint Act (abolition of the cent)

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Currency Act and the Royal Canadian Mint Act to provide that one cent coins will not be legal tender beginning on January 1 of the year immediately following the year in which the enactment is assented to, and will be called in following a proclamation by the Governor in Council.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Currency and Royal Canadian Mint (abolition of the cent)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Currency Act and the Royal Canadian Mint Act (abolition of the cent)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Section 7 of the Currency Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Para.change: (3) Notwithstanding subsection (1), beginning on January 1 of the year immediately following the year in which this Act receives royal assent, a coin shall not pass current.

Block.Para: 2. (1) Subsection 8(2) of the Act is amended by adding the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (c), by striking out the word âandâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by repealing paragraph (e).
(2) Section 8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Para.change: (2.01) Beginning on January 1 of the year immediately following the year in which this Act receives royal assent, every amount payable in cash shall be rounded as follows:
(a) 1 and 2 cents, rounded down to the nearest 10 cents;
(b) 3 and 4 cents, rounded up to the nearest 5 cents;
(c) 6 and 7 cents, rounded down to the nearest 5 cents; and
(d) 8 and 9 cents, rounded up to the nearest 10 cents.

Block.Para: 3. Subsection 9(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Block.MarginalNote: Calling in and ceasing production of coins of one cent

Para.change: (a) shall, not later than January 1 of the year immediately following the year in which this Act receives royal assent, by proclamation, call in all coins of a denomination of one cent, in exchange for coins or notes of a denomination of five cents or more; and

Block.Para: (b) may, by proclamation, call in any other coins of any date and denomination.
4. Section 6.4 of the Royal Canadian Mint Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Para.change: (3) Any order made under subsection (1) in respect of a one cent coin is revoked and without further effect.

Block.Para: 5. Items 7 to 9 of Part 2 of the schedule to the Act are repealed.

Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
